title: CLI control-surface activation
scope_in: CLI command surface that calls devmasterd HTTP APIs
intake command submitting POST /v1/intake with a local task proposal JSON
smoke command that exercises intake -> authorize -> run-provider -> evidence against localhost devmasterd
token supplied by DEVMASTERD_TOKEN or explicit test-only flag in smoke tests

proposed_sequence.purpose: Review this proposal before any CLI implementation starts.
Implement a local CLI client for the existing localhost devmasterd API.
Review CLI smoke evidence from a real localhost devmasterd instance.

task_proposal.goal: Add the first CLI client surface for the validated localhost devmasterd kernel.
task_proposal.rationale: Gate J validated the daemon kernel. The next lowest-risk product surface is a CLI client that consumes the daemon API without becoming a second control plane.

acceptance_checks: CLI remains a daemon API consumer and does not own queue, authorization, cost, ratchet, or evidence policy.
CLI smoke runs against localhost devmasterd with token auth.
CLI does not call real providers, subscription CLIs, live auto_router, external repos, deploy systems, or PR APIs.
CLI validation writes a runtime artifact proving status, intake, authorization, provider fixture execution, and evidence readback.

risk_controls.mitigation: CLI may only call devmasterd endpoints and may not write runtime queue/evidence files directly.
CLI must read token from environment and must not persist token values in logs, evidence, or state artifacts.

risk_controls.risk: surface creep into Web or IDE work
risk_controls.mitigation: Gate K and Phase 8 scope explicitly exclude Web Console and IDE extension.

recommendation: Proceed to Gate K review. Do not implement CLI until Gate K confirms this proposal keeps CLI as a thin devmasterd client with no real provider, external repo, deploy, PR, Web, or IDE scope.
